name: Multigrain
description: Live stereo morphing granular sampler with 8 Sounds, two morphable Scenes per Sound, three assignable CV inputs, built-in Blur reverb, looping recorder for live input, and microSD sample storage.

controls.name: START
controls.description: Starting position of each grain within the sample or live memory.
controls.detail: Default: 0 sec · full range across sample length

controls.name: WRAP
controls.description: Defines the scan area width from the START position. Controls how far grains can stray.

controls.name: SCAN
controls.description: Speed and direction of automatic position scanning through the sample.
controls.detail: CCW = reverse scan · center = no scan · CW = forward scan

controls.name: SHAPE
controls.description: Grain window/envelope shape. Controls the amplitude envelope applied to each grain.
controls.detail: 7 discrete options: Bell, Sinc, Triangle, Decay, Ramp, Square, Tukey (default)

controls.name: BLUR
controls.description: Reverb send amount per grain. Controls decay amount of the built-in Blur reverb effect.

controls.name: LEVEL
controls.description: Grain volume level. Sets the amplitude of generated grains.

controls.name: RATE
controls.description: Grain trigger rate — how frequently new grains are generated.

controls.name: SIZE
controls.description: Grain length. Duration of each individual grain.

controls.name: PITCH
controls.description: Grain playback pitch/speed. Transposes grain playback relative to original sample pitch.
controls.detail: Default: 1x (unity) · tracks V/Oct via SELECT or GATE normalling

controls.name: TONE
controls.description: 2-pole LP–HP filter applied per grain. Shapes timbral content of each grain independently.

controls.name: MORPH Fader
controls.description: Crossfades all parameters between Scene A and Scene B simultaneously for smooth timbral morphing.
controls.detail: Left = Scene A · Right = Scene B · LED shows modulated position

controls.name: MORPH ATT
controls.description: Attenuverter for the MORPH CV input. Controls level and inversion of incoming CV.
controls.detail: MORPH fader acts as offset when CV present

inputs.name: IN L + IN R
inputs.description: Stereo audio input for live granular processing via Looping Recorder.

inputs.name: NEXT
inputs.description: Trigger advances to next Sound (>2V). Normalled to GATE.

inputs.name: GATE
inputs.description: Gate input triggers the currently playing Sound. While high, grains generate at RATE.

inputs.name: X
inputs.description: Assignable modulation CV input 1. Assign to any granular parameter per Scene.

inputs.name: Y
inputs.description: Assignable modulation CV input 2. Assign to any granular parameter per Scene.

inputs.name: Z
inputs.description: Assignable modulation CV input 3. Assign to any granular parameter per Scene.

inputs.name: SELECT
inputs.description: CV selects active Sound (0–5V maps to Sounds 1–8). Negative voltage mirrored.

inputs.name: MORPH CV
inputs.description: CV input for MORPH fader position. Scaled by MORPH ATT attenuverter.

inputs.name: SYNC
inputs.description: Clock/trigger input for synchronizing grain rate to external clock.

outputs.name: OUT L
outputs.description: Left stereo audio output of granular engine (includes Blur reverb).

outputs.name: OUT R
outputs.description: Right stereo audio output of granular engine (includes Blur reverb).

outputs.name: A
outputs.description: Assignable CV output 1. Routable to various internal parameters.

outputs.name: B
outputs.description: Assignable CV output 2. Routable to various internal parameters.

behaviors.name: Granular Playback
behaviors.trigger: SOUND button press or GATE input
behaviors.description: Press or hold a SOUND 1-8 button to generate grains from that Sound's sample at the set RATE. All 8 Sounds can overlap simultaneously. LATCH button sustains grain generation continuously.

behaviors.name: Scene Morphing
behaviors.description: Each Sound stores two Scenes (A and B) with independent knob positions. MORPH fader smoothly interpolates all parameters simultaneously. REVERSE and SHAPE morph probabilistically.

behaviors.name: Live Sound (Looping Recorder)
behaviors.description: Assigns a Sound to live audio input. Looping Recorder captures up to 32 seconds from IN L/R. Granular engine processes live memory in real time.

behaviors.name: Modulation Assignment
behaviors.trigger: press RAND / X / Y / Z button
behaviors.description: Assign X, Y, Z CV inputs or internal random to any granular parameter. Attenuversion amounts stored per Scene and morph between Scenes.

behaviors.name: Sample Loading
behaviors.description: Browse and load stereo WAV files from microSD card (≥32GB recommended). Projects and Presets save complete configurations including Scenes and mod assignments.

patchIdeas.name: Ambient drone cloud
patchIdeas.patch: Latch a sustaining pad Sound with long grains, slow rate, Bell shape and heavy BLUR for a self-running drone.
steps: Load a sustaining pad/chord WAV on SOUND 1 (ALT → /WAVS → pick a sample).
Set SIZE ~2 o'clock (~2 sec) and SHAPE to Bell.
Set RATE ~10 o'clock (around 1 Hz). Unlink RATE–SIZE if linked so grains don't all stack.
Set BLUR ~3 o'clock for a long reverb tail.
Set LEVEL at noon, TONE at noon, PITCH at noon.
Press LATCH, then tap SOUND 1 — grains keep firing without holding the button.
Patch OUT L → Mixer L, OUT R → Mixer R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — continuous stereo granular pad with long reverb tail, audio level (~10Vpp).
patchIdeas.hear: A sustained pad that keeps flowing without any trigger — long Bell-shaped grains from the sample bloom into the Blur reverb. Press LATCH again to stop; grains finish then Blur decays away.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MULTIGRAIN:
* SIZE: 2-4s
* RATE: 1-2Hz
* SHAPE: Bell
* BLUR: high
* LATCH: ON
- MULTIGRAIN (OUT L) -> MIXER (In L)
- MULTIGRAIN (OUT R) -> MIXER (In R) // self-sustaining drone, no external trigger needed

patchIdeas.name: Rhythmic grain percussion
patchIdeas.patch: Sync RATE to a clock and use short grains over a drum loop so Multigrain rearranges the beat one grain per pulse.
steps: Load a drum or percussion loop on SOUND 1.
Press ALT, then press SYNC so SYNC lights YELLOW (RATE is now clock-synced).
Press RATE–SIZE link so RATE multiplies the clock and SIZE snaps to clock divisions.
Set SIZE ~10 o'clock (100–200 ms short grains).
Set RATE around 2 o'clock for 2x–4x the clock rate.
Set SCAN just CW of center for slow forward scanning through the loop.
Patch your master clock → SYNC. Patch a gate → GATE to play SOUND 1.
Patch OUT L → Mixer L, OUT R → Mixer R.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — short clock-synced grains chopping the drum loop, audio level.
patchIdeas.hear: Your drum loop reshuffled into a tight stutter that stays locked to the clock. Turn SCAN further CW to walk through the loop, or CCW for reversed ordering.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MULTIGRAIN:
* SIZE: 100-200ms
* SCAN: forward
* RATE-SIZE link: 2-4x
- CLOCK (Out) c> MULTIGRAIN (SYNC)
- MULTIGRAIN (OUT L) -> MIXER (In L)
- MULTIGRAIN (OUT R) -> MIXER (In R) // rate locks to external clock

patchIdeas.name: Scene morph macro
patchIdeas.patch: One slow LFO into MORPH CV crossfades every knob between Scene A and Scene B — a one-cable macro control.
steps: On SOUND 1, press SCENE A and dial a calm state (SIZE noon, BLUR low, PITCH noon, SHAPE Tukey, RATE ~11 o'clock).
Press SCENE B and dial a contrasting state (SIZE ~3 o'clock, BLUR ~3 o'clock, PITCH +1 oct, SHAPE Bell, RATE ~2 o'clock).
Slide MORPH fader to the left (Scene A) as the starting position.
Set MORPH ATT to ~2 o'clock.
Patch a slow LFO (≈0.05–0.2 Hz, ±5V) → MORPH CV.
Press LATCH + SOUND 1 so grains keep firing.
Patch OUT L / OUT R → Mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — continuously morphing granular voice, audio level.
patchIdeas.hear: Every parameter breathes between the two Scenes in step with the LFO: grain size, pitch, envelope shape and reverb all evolve together. REVERSE and SHAPE flip probabilistically at intermediate MORPH positions.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MULTIGRAIN:
* Scene A / Scene B: contrasting settings
* MORPH ATT: ~2 o'clock
- LFO (Out) >> MULTIGRAIN (MORPH CV)
- MULTIGRAIN (OUT L) -> MIXER (In L)
- MULTIGRAIN (OUT R) -> MIXER (In R) // one cable morphs all params

patchIdeas.name: Live granular looper
patchIdeas.patch: Assign a Sound to the Looping Recorder so incoming audio is granulated in real time, with optional THRSH auto-freeze.
steps: Hold SOUND 1 and press SAMPLE — SOUND 1 turns MAGENTA (now a Live Sound, Looping Recorder active).
Patch a source → IN L, and its right channel → IN R (or leave R unpatched for mono).
In ALT → SAMPLE settings, set GAIN so audio registers cleanly; optionally set THRSH so the recorder auto-freezes when the input stops.
Set START full CW (newest audio), WRAP ~2 o'clock, SCAN at noon.
Set SIZE ~noon (~1 sec), SHAPE Tukey, RATE ~noon, BLUR ~11 o'clock.
Press LATCH + SOUND 1 so grains run continuously from live memory.
Press SAMPLE to Freeze/Unfreeze the buffer (button turns CYAN when frozen).
Patch OUT L / OUT R → Mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — granular rework of the last 8–32 seconds of live input, audio level.
patchIdeas.hear: Whatever you feed in is sliced into grains in real time. Freeze (SAMPLE) to lock the recent input as a static loop; unfreeze to keep capturing. THRSH auto-freezes on silence so pauses hold the previous phrase.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MULTIGRAIN:
* SOUND: Live mode
* Looping Recorder: armed
- SOURCE (Out L) -> MULTIGRAIN (IN L)
- SOURCE (Out R) -> MULTIGRAIN (IN R)
- MULTIGRAIN (OUT L) -> MIXER (In L)
- MULTIGRAIN (OUT R) -> MIXER (In R) // live input granularized in realtime

patchIdeas.name: Probability shape mixing
patchIdeas.patch: SHAPE is discrete so MORPH becomes a probability crossfader between Scene A's shape and Scene B's shape, grain by grain.
steps: On SOUND 1, press SCENE A and set SHAPE to Tukey. Leave other knobs where you like them.
Press SCENE B and set SHAPE to Decay. Match the other knobs to Scene A.
Set MORPH fader about 25% of the way from left (Scene A) toward right (Scene B).
Set SIZE ~noon, RATE ~1 o'clock so new grains fire often enough to hear the mix.
Press LATCH + SOUND 1.
Patch OUT L / OUT R → Mixer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: OUT L / OUT R — granular stream where each grain is Tukey or Decay with set probability, audio level.
patchIdeas.hear: About 75% of grains use the smooth Tukey envelope and 25% use the sharper Decay envelope — the overall mix stays steady but individual grains fade or click differently. Slide MORPH to rebias the ratio.
patchIdeas.diagram:
MULTIGRAIN:
* Scene A SHAPE: Tukey
* Scene B SHAPE: Decay
* MORPH fader: 25% toward B
- MULTIGRAIN (OUT L) -> MIXER (In L)
- MULTIGRAIN (OUT R) -> MIXER (In R) // shape chosen probabilistically per grain
